modules.compliance_anchor: Landcom Blue Book Vol 1, 4th Edition - Section 6.3.2

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Is the water overtopping the fabric, or is the fence physically bowing under the weight?
scenarios.technical_specs: Max catchment: 0.6ha per 100m. Max pole spacing: 2.5m.

branches.overtopping: Check the catchment area. A single sediment fence can only handle 0.6 hectares per 100 meters. If catchment is too large, install an additional fence upslope.
branches.bowing: Check your star picket spacing. They must be spaced no further than 2.5 meters apart. Install extra pickets immediately to support the weight.

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Was the geofabric trenched 150mm into the soil and backfilled, or just pinned to the surface?
scenarios.technical_specs: Trenching requirement: 150mm deep, backfilled and compacted.

branches.pinned: The installation has failed. You must re-trench the fabric 150mm deep and backfill it with compacted soil to create a seal.

modules.compliance_anchor: Landcom Blue Book Vol 1, 4th Edition - Section 4.3.2

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Has this stockpile been inactive for more than 10 days, and is there a sediment fence installed on the downslope?
scenarios.technical_specs: Inactive stockpiles (>10 days) must be covered or stabilized.

branches.inactive_no_fence: Violation. Stockpiles inactive for > 10 days must be covered or seeded, and must have a sediment fence on the downslope side. Fix immediately.
branches.active_no_fence: Even if active, you must install a sediment fence 2 meters from the downslope base to catch runoff.
branches.inactive_with_fence: Fence is good, but if it's inactive for > 10 days, you still need to apply a polymer dust binder or hydromulch to lock it down.

modules.compliance_anchor: Landcom Blue Book Vol 1, 4th Edition - Section 2.2

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Is the Erosion and Sediment Control Plan (ESCP) displayed at the site office or available digitally?
scenarios.technical_specs: Legislative requirement: ESCP must be current and on-site for all disturbed areas > 2500m2.

branches.missing: Major non-compliance. Stop work until the ESCP is located or a new one is drafted by the site engineer.
branches.available: Good. Ensure all contractors have been briefed on the specific controls for this stage.

modules.compliance_anchor: Landcom Blue Book Vol 1, 4th Edition - Section 8.2

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Has it rained more than 5mm in the last 24 hours, and have you checked the sediment levels in all trapped areas?
scenarios.technical_specs: Inspection required: Weekly and within 24 hours of rain > 5mm.

branches.no_inspection: Violation. Conduct a full site walk-through now and document any repairs needed in the site log.
branches.complete: Ensure any sediment removed is placed in a stabilized stockpile area, not near drains.

modules.compliance_anchor: Protection of the Environment Operations (POEO) Act 1997

scenarios.diagnostic_question: Does the site have a dedicated logbook of all ESC maintenance and is the self-audit up to date?
scenarios.technical_specs: POEO Act: Site supervisors must provide evidence of due diligence to avoid environmental harm.

branches.no_logbook: High Risk. The EPA can issue 'Clean Up' notices or fines on the spot if due diligence (maintenance logs) cannot be proven.
branches.logbook_ready: Present the logbook to the inspector. Ensure all previous non-conformances have been signed off as repaired.
